market_context: Markets face mixed signals with <cite index="1-6,1-9">Apple jumping 3% in premarket after better-than-expected quarterly results</cite> while <cite index="1-2,1-5">Nasdaq futures slipped 0.1% after April's best month since 2020</cite>. <cite index="32-10,32-11">Oil prices remain elevated with July Brent futures rising 0.9% to $111.34 and WTI flat at $105.07</cite> amid ongoing Iran tensions despite the ceasefire.

predictions.reasoning: With Dow futures up 0.3% and S&P futures up 0.1%, positive momentum from Apple's strong earnings should lift the broader market. The VIX declined 8.8% over 5 days suggesting reduced fear, supporting an upward move despite geopolitical headwinds.

Apple beat earnings with $2.01 EPS vs $1.96 expected and revenue of $111.2B vs $109.66B expected. Strong iPhone sales and 17% revenue growth, plus shares already up 3% in premarket, indicate continued strength. New CEO transition is viewed positively by markets.

Nasdaq futures down 0.1% despite Apple's strong results suggests tech sector facing headwinds. Oil prices rising above $111 create inflation concerns that particularly hurt high-multiple tech stocks. Profit-taking likely after April's 15.3% gain.

Oil prices surging with Brent at $111.34 and WTI holding $105 levels due to ongoing Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. Trump maintaining blockade stance creates sustained upward pressure on energy sector despite ceasefire.

Rising oil prices to $111+ levels threaten to reignite inflation concerns, putting pressure on bonds. Strong Apple earnings and positive economic data reduce safe-haven demand for treasuries, with 10-year yield at 4.39% already elevated.
